warnings: This is a Sources Sought notice, not a solicitation. The response deadline is for market research submissions, not contract proposals.

achievements: Reduced document processing time by 80%, from 8-12 hours to 1.5-2 hours
Improved data extraction accuracy to 95%, virtually eliminating manual rework
Platform processes documents from over 500 institutions simultaneously without latency
Delivered over $800,000 in annual labor cost savings
Achieved 180% ROI within 14 months of launch
User satisfaction scores increased by over 40%
Ensured SLA compliance even during peak academic seasons
project.description: Designed and implemented a serverless enterprise architecture using over 20 AWS services that enabled DegreeData to automate academic document processing, credential verification, and transcript evaluation. The platform transformed document-heavy workflows into a scalable, automated, and intelligent system serving over 2,500 colleges, universities, and professional organizations globally.
project.title: Enterprise Educational Data Platform
project.technicalApproach: Built serverless compute layer with AWS Lambda and AWS Fargate orchestrated through Step Functions for sequential and parallel workflows. Integrated Amazon Textract for structured data extraction from scanned transcripts and forms with over 95% accuracy including handwritten notes. AWS Comprehend provided NLP to classify and extract academic attributes (course titles, grades, GPAs). Amazon Bedrock powered a custom document parser and RAG model to convert unstructured data into JSON for RDS ingestion. Amazon OpenSearch enabled real-time search across millions of academic records. Amazon QuickSight provided dynamic dashboards for processing queues, data quality metrics, and SLA monitoring. Data layer used RDS MySQL for transactional data, DynamoDB for semi-structured records and metadata, S3 for document storage, and ElastiCache for institutional templates. Security via WAF, Shield, Certificate Manager, KMS, Secrets Manager. API Gateway and AppSync for real-time client access. EventBridge and SQS/SNS for async microservice communication. CI/CD with CloudFormation, CodePipeline, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy.

achievements: Reduced loan processing time by 65%, from 5-7 business days to 1.5-2 days
Achieved 92% accuracy in handwriting recognition
Reduced document resubmission by 78%
Delivered 40% operational cost savings ($2.1M annually)
Achieved 99.9% uptime with 10x improved scalability
Increased customer satisfaction scores by 35%
Reduced regulatory review preparation time by 60%
25% increase in loan origination capacity without additional headcount
project.description: Architected and implemented a comprehensive serverless loan processing platform that transformed BMO's document-heavy workflow into an intelligent, automated system. The solution leveraged AWS Lambda, Step Functions, Textract, Comprehend, and Amazon Bedrock to process commercial loan applications, reducing processing time by 65% and achieving 92% accuracy on handwritten content extraction.
project.title: Enterprise Loan Processing Platform
project.technicalApproach: Built event-driven serverless architecture using AWS Lambda as core compute engine with Step Functions for workflow orchestration. Implemented Amazon Textract for document intelligence extracting text, tables, and form data from loan applications and financial statements. Used Amazon Comprehend for NLP analysis of unstructured text and Amazon Bedrock for intelligent document classification. Data layer used Amazon RDS PostgreSQL for transactional data with DynamoDB for document metadata and real-time tracking. Security implemented via VPC, WAF, Shield, KMS, and Secrets Manager.

achievements: 60% reduction in document routing time
98.6% classification accuracy through iterative AI tuning with human-in-the-loop feedback
40% reduction in manual headcount for mailroom and intake departments
Improved SLA compliance across all 12 document-handling departments
Automatic scaling during peak processing periods with robust error handling
40% more documents processed with same operational overhead
Project completed ahead of schedule and within scope
project.description: Developed an AI-powered automation platform for Ford Credit to manage the classification and routing of high volumes of scanned physical correspondence across 12 departments. The solution leveraged AWS machine learning services to completely automate document classification and routing, reducing routing time by 60% and achieving 98.6% classification accuracy through iterative human-in-the-loop feedback mechanisms.
project.title: AI-Powered Document Classification & Routing System
project.technicalApproach: Built intelligent document routing system using Amazon Textract as primary document analysis engine for extracting text and structured data from scanned letters, forms, and documents regardless of format, quality, or handwriting variations. Amazon Comprehend provided NLP to understand document context, sentiment, and intent for classification. Amazon A2I (Augmented AI) enabled human-in-the-loop validation for complex or ambiguous documents with feedback mechanisms for continuous model improvement. AWS Lambda and Step Functions handled orchestration and processing logic. Amazon S3 for secure document storage with encryption, DynamoDB for real-time metadata tracking, MySQL/RDS for logging, auditing, and metrics. Documents classified into categories (payment inquiries, loan modifications, insurance claims, complaints, service requests) and automatically routed to departmental queues. Full-cycle AI project including data preprocessing, model training/refinement, deployment, and integration with existing case management systems via RESTful APIs.

achievements: Automated lead capture and enrichment pipeline
CRM integration with ServiceNow for seamless funnel tracking
Enhanced lead quality through external API enrichment
project.description: Integrated lead capture and analysis with external services for enhanced sales funnel tracking. The platform connects a React frontend with a Python backend, external enrichment APIs, and CRM integration.
project.title: Lead Capture & Analysis Integration Platform
project.technicalApproach: Built a React frontend with Python backend. Integrated Hunter.io API for lead enrichment and email verification. Connected with ServiceNow for CRM and ticket management integration.

achievements: Cost-effective job platform for SMB market
Streamlined CI/CD with GitLab and Amplify
Serverless architecture reduced operational costs
project.description: Built an Amplify-hosted job platform with streamlined deployments and cost savings for small and medium businesses. The platform provides job listing, application management, and employer dashboards.
project.title: Amplify-Hosted Job Platform
project.technicalApproach: Developed with GitLab CI/CD integrated with AWS Amplify for automated deployments. Built a serverless backend with S3 and CloudFront for static asset hosting and global content delivery.

recommendations: Overall past performance coverage is below 70%. This may significantly impact win probability.

data.narrativeSummary: Found 5 relevant past performance project(s). The strongest match is "Enterprise Educational Data Platform" with a 44% relevance score. Overall past performance coverage: 0%.

competitiveAdvantages: AWS Advanced Consulting Partner with 16 AWS-certified professionals and $7.5M+ in completed AWS engagements
Demonstrated FedRAMP and GovCloud expertise with documented shared responsibility model and security architecture
Proven AI/ML and LLM deployment capabilities including Amazon Bedrock, Textract, and Comprehend in production environments
Existing knowledge base of compliance documentation generation, ATO support, and federal security standards (NIST 800-53, FISMA, HIPAA)
DGS California Small Business (Micro) certification demonstrating small business commitment
Bottom-up fully burdened labor rates with no hidden surcharges and AWS pass-through at cost
Past performance in enterprise-scale serverless architecture with 20+ AWS services per engagement and 99.9% uptime track record
data.basisOfEstimate: Bottom-up estimate constructed from the Draft SOW's 3-phase, 8-month core implementation plus optional 4-month post-transition support period. Labor estimated across 8 key roles: Cloud Solutions Architect (1 FTE, 12 months, ~1920 hrs at ~$250/hr), Security/Compliance Lead (1 FTE, 12 months, ~1920 hrs at ~$230/hr), DevSecOps Engineer (2 FTEs, 10 months, ~3200 hrs at ~$200/hr), AI/ML Engineer for LLM deployment (1 FTE, 8 months, ~1280 hrs at ~$220/hr), Data Engineer for ETL pipelines (1 FTE, 6 months, ~960 hrs at ~$190/hr), Compliance Analyst (1 FTE, 8 months, ~1280 hrs at ~$170/hr), Training Specialist (1 FTE, 3 months, ~480 hrs at ~$160/hr), and Program Manager (1 FTE, 12 months, ~1920 hrs at ~$210/hr). Total labor hours estimated at approximately 12,960 across all roles yielding ~$3.65M in labor. Material costs of $485,000 include estimated AWS GovCloud infrastructure (GPU instances, storage, networking) at ~$300K, LLM licensing and tooling at ~$100K, and Splunk/Palo Alto/Voltage integration licensing at ~$85K. Indirect costs of $375,000 cover overhead, G&A, and facilities. Profit margin of 10% applied. No government estimate was available as this is a Sources Sought notice; estimate is benchmarked against comparable federal CCoE and FedRAMP implementation contracts in the $3M-$7M range for similar scope and duration. Organization-provided labor rates ($171-$179/hr) were used as a floor but adjusted upward to reflect the specialized FedRAMP High, Zero Trust, and AI/ML expertise required by this SOW.
assumptions: This is a Sources Sought notice — no contract will be awarded from this notice; pricing is a rough order of magnitude for planning purposes only
Period of performance is 1 base year: 8 months core implementation plus 4 months optional post-transition support
All work performed remotely as stated in the SOW; no travel costs included
AWS GovCloud infrastructure costs are estimated as pass-through at approximately $25K/month including GPU-enabled instances for LLM hosting
Team size of approximately 8-10 FTEs across the performance period with varying levels of effort per phase
Public Trust clearances are obtainable for all proposed personnel within project timeline
Subcontractor support will be needed for Palo Alto Cortex, Splunk Enterprise Security, and Voltage SecureData integration — estimated at 15-20% of total labor
LLM licensing costs assume open-source or government-licensed models deployed in secure containers rather than commercial SaaS AI services
The eventual solicitation may be set aside under the Buy Indian Act, which could require teaming with an IEE prime — this would alter the pricing structure and margin allocation
Organization labor rates were adjusted upward from provided rates to reflect market rates for FedRAMP High and Zero Trust specialized expertise required by this SOW
No existing GSA Schedule or SEWP vehicle is assumed; acquisition vehicle costs not included
Contract type assumed to be T&M or hybrid based on the CLIN structure with optional T&M CLIN 005 for Resident Architect support
pricingRisks: Buy Indian Act set-aside: IHS strongly prefers Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) and Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEE) — Horus Technology does not appear to qualify as IEE/ISBEE, which may disqualify the firm entirely if the acquisition is set aside
No government estimated value provided — this is a Sources Sought notice, not a solicitation, making price benchmarking highly uncertain
Contract type is unknown; pricing strategy may need to shift significantly between FFP, T&M, or cost-plus depending on eventual solicitation structure

Scope is extremely broad: cloud landing zone, FedRAMP High ATO, Zero Trust, LLM deployment, SIEM/SOAR integration, ETL pipelines, compliance dashboards, and full training — risk of underestimating effort
Requirement for Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, and Voltage SecureData integration may require specialized subcontractor support outside current team capabilities
Public Trust clearance requirement for all personnel may limit available staffing pool and increase labor costs
GPU-enabled container environments for enclave-hosted LLMs carry significant and unpredictable cloud infrastructure costs

Organization labor rates provided (Software Engineer $171.40/hr, Manufacturing Engineer $178.89/hr) are below typical federal cloud/security architect rates of $200-$300/hr, potentially compressing margins on senior roles
Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) readiness and Agentic AI capabilities are emerging requirements with limited market precedent for pricing
recommendedActions: CRITICAL: Assess Buy Indian Act eligibility — if Horus Technology cannot qualify as IEE/ISBEE, consider teaming arrangement with a qualified Indian Economic Enterprise as prime contractor
Submit a capabilities response to this Sources Sought to establish presence and gain intelligence for the eventual solicitation
Identify and engage subcontractors for Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, and Voltage SecureData integration expertise
Develop a detailed staffing plan mapping SOW roles (Cloud Architect, Security Engineer, DevSecOps, LLM/AI Engineer, Compliance Analyst, Training Lead) to internal and subcontractor resources
Obtain refined fully burdened rates for federal cloud security work — current org rates appear to reflect commercial/general IT rates rather than specialized FedRAMP/ZTA rates
Estimate AWS GovCloud infrastructure costs separately as pass-through using AWS pricing calculator for EC2 GPU instances, S3, VPC, PrivateLink, and other required services
Monitor SAM.gov for the eventual solicitation release and prepare a compliant proposal shell aligned with the 5 CLIN structure
Pursue GSA Schedule or NASA SEWP contract vehicle if not already held, as the solicitation specifically asks about existing governmentwide vehicles
Engage with IHS stakeholders or attend any industry day events to refine understanding of scope, budget, and evaluation criteria

evaluationFactors: Timeliness of deliverables (±10 business days tolerance)
Accuracy and usability of ATO documentation
Compliance of enclave with security benchmarks
Training satisfaction scores (≥90% competency pass rate)
Risk dashboards maintained with ≤24hr lag from telemetry source
Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE) or Indian Small Business Economic Enterprise (ISBEE) status under Buy Indian Act preference
data.overview: The Indian Health Service (IHS), through HHS/OMAS, is conducting market research via a Sources Sought notice to identify prospective sources—particularly Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEE) and Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE)—capable of providing cloud hosting, provisioning, and management services to establish a Secure Cloud Center of Excellence (CCoE). The requirement centers on a FedRAMP High-authorized IaaS/PaaS cloud platform (GovCloud) with at least 170 compliant services, supporting federal healthcare operations with multi-region U.S.-only deployment, tribal data sovereignty, Zero Trust Architecture, AI/LLM enablement, compliance automation, and integration with existing IHS security tools (Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, Voltage SecureData). This is NOT a solicitation; responses inform acquisition strategy and potential set-aside determinations under the Buy Indian Act.

requirements.requirement: Provide FedRAMP High-authorized IaaS cloud platform with minimum 170 compliant services
All physical infrastructure (compute, storage, networking) must be located within CONUS
Design and deploy Cloud Landing Zone with multi-account governance using Control Tower, Service Catalog, and Organizations
Provision secure enclaves for development, staging, and production workloads with CIS-hardened configurations
Implement Identity and Access Management aligned with Zero Trust principles per CISA ZTMM
Integrate with Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM and Cortex XSOAR for Security Operations Center
Integrate with Splunk Enterprise Security for SIEM workflows
Integrate with Voltage SecureData for Zero Trust Architecture Data Pillar implementation
Provide Agentic AI capabilities to enable 60-70% reduction in manual effort
Deploy GPU-enabled container environments for enclave-hosted LLMs supporting documentation generation and compliance analytics
Build ETL pipelines for ingesting and transforming log/event data with PHI/PII masking per HIPAA and FISMA
Automate ATO lifecycle management including POA&M tracking, patch validation, and compliance reporting
Deploy real-time compliance dashboards for executive reporting on ATO progress, vulnerabilities, and audit readiness
Implement microsegmentation, encrypted remote access, and continuous validation per Zero Trust Architecture
Provide centralized log aggregation, threat detection via SIEM, EDR, and vulnerability management
Implement encrypted storage with NIST 800-60 aligned tagging, classification, and retention policies
Support multi-region deployment with U.S.-only operations maintaining tribal data sovereignty
Deploy AI-assisted document generation for SSP, SAR, RAR, POA&M with no data egress from enclave
Integrate with ServiceNow or equivalent ticketing system for security incident workflows
Comply with FedRAMP High Baseline throughout all deployed systems
Comply with NIST 800-53 Rev 5 security controls
Align with Executive Order 14028 and OMB M-22-09, M-24-04 directives
Implement CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model (ZTMM) across all ZTA pillars
Ensure Post Quantum Computing (PQC) readiness and mandate enablement
All data must remain in GovCloud (US) including LLM operations—no data egress
All personnel accessing tribal health data must be U.S. persons subject to federal oversight and background investigation
Public Trust background clearance required for all contractor personnel
NDAs required for all contractor personnel accessing sensitive IHS data
Maintain risk dashboards with ≤24hr lag from telemetry source
Comply with HIPAA and FISMA for PHI/PII data handling
Achieve continuous compliance monitoring and FedRAMP alignment across all systems
Provide weekly status updates, risk registers, and action items
Provide monthly executive dashboards and cost transparency reports
Provide quarterly performance and compliance reviews with stakeholders
Deliver all ATO documentation (SSP, SAP, SAR, POA&M) for formal submission
If claiming ISBEE or IEE status, submit completed IHS IEE Representation Form
Must be registered with System for Award Management (SAM)
Complete all core implementation phases within one-year Base Period of Performance
Deliver Cloud Landing Zone Architecture and Configuration Artifacts
Deliver validated automation playbooks for ATO, POA&M, Patch, and IR workflows
Deliver role-based training curriculum for ISSOs, DevSecOps, Security Analysts, System Owners, and Auditors
Deliver SOPs for enclave management, ETL, compliance automation, and LLM use
Deliver knowledge transfer package with architecture, workflows, and configuration artifacts
Deliver operational readiness checklist and transition acceptance memo
Provide Key Personnel with demonstrated experience supporting U.S. public sector cloud security, automation, and compliance modernization
All Key Personnel must be cleared to Public Trust level or higher and remain assigned throughout performance period
Provide role-specific interfaces and toolkits for ISSOs, POA&M Analysts, Auditors, and System Owners
All work performed remotely unless otherwise directed by Contracting Officer using IHS-approved virtual collaboration platforms
Preference given to Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) under Buy Indian Act 25 U.S.C. 47
NAICS code 541519 - Other Computer Related Services applies to this acquisition
Implement cost accountability framework via chargeback model supported by Cloud Budgets and Cost Explorer

format: Responses must be sufficiently detailed to demonstrate qualifications
Do not include sensitive or proprietary information
Telephone inquiries will not be accepted

deliverables: Cloud Landing Zone Architecture and Configuration Artifacts
IAM and VPC Security Configuration Documentation
Hardened ETL Server Deployment Package and Logging Setup

Initial LLM Hosting Blueprint with Secure Container Deployment
Draft ATO Documents: SSP, SAP, RAR, Continuous Monitoring Strategy
Cloud Governance and Risk Register (Initial Baseline)
Validated Automation Playbooks for ATO, POA&M, Patch, and IR workflows
Deployed Compliance Dashboards with Export/Reporting Capabilities
Integrated SIEM/EDR/Vulnerability Dashboard and Alerting Interfaces
Enclave-Based LLM Toolkits: Prompt Libraries, Summary Engines, Audit RFI Generator
Peer-Reviewed Codebase and QA Documentation for Security Automations

UAT Report, Change Logs, and Final QA Sign-off
Training Curriculum by Role (ISSOs, DevSecOps, Security Analysts, etc.)
Admin Manuals, Reference Guides, and Recorded Training Sessions
SOPs for Enclave Management, ETL, Compliance Automation, and LLM Use
Knowledge Transfer Package with Architecture, Workflows, and Configuration Artifacts
Operational Readiness Checklist and Transition Acceptance Memo
Final Compliance Snapshot Report (ZTMM, NIST 800-53, FedRAMP metrics)
Post-Implementation Sustainability and Improvement Plan
Weekly Status Updates and Risk Registers
Monthly Executive Dashboards and Cost Transparency Reports
Quarterly Performance and Compliance Reviews

redFlags.whyItMatters: The entire purpose of this sources sought is to determine availability of ISBEE and IEE firms. If sufficient IEE/ISBEE firms respond, the solicitation will almost certainly be set aside under the Buy Indian Act, making non-IEE firms ineligible as prime contractors. This is the single most significant go/no-go factor.
redFlags.mitigation: If HORUSTECH is not an IEE/ISBEE, the only viable path is as a subcontractor to a qualified IEE/ISBEE prime. Identify and begin outreach to potential IEE/ISBEE teaming partners immediately.
redFlags.flag: Buy Indian Act set-aside likely excludes non-IEE firms from prime competition

redFlags.whyItMatters: The level of AWS-specific detail (Control Tower, Organizations, Service Catalog, EC2, S3, Glacier IR, Deep Archive, PrivateLink, CloudTrail, GuardDuty, Config, Security Hub, Cost Explorer) suggests the requirement may have been developed with a specific vendor or solution in mind. This could indicate an incumbent or preferred vendor advantage.
redFlags.mitigation: In the sources sought response, note the AWS-specific language and request clarification on whether alternative FedRAMP High platforms would be considered. This positions HORUSTECH as engaged while flagging potential competition concerns.
redFlags.flag: SOW is written around AWS GovCloud with no 'or equivalent' language — potential wired specification

redFlags.whyItMatters: The convergence of these specialized requirements suggests the government may already have a specific vendor or very small pool in mind. Firms without existing IHS or tribal health IT experience may face a steep disadvantage even if technically capable.
redFlags.mitigation: Assess honestly whether HORUSTECH can credibly demonstrate experience across all requirement areas. If gaps exist in more than two major areas, consider a no-bid or subcontractor role.
redFlags.flag: Combination of niche requirements (FedRAMP High + tribal data sovereignty + specific security tools + LLM in secure enclaves) narrows competitive field significantly

redFlags.whyItMatters: Knowledge base and past performance searches returned no relevant federal healthcare, IHS, or tribal health IT engagements. Without demonstrable past performance in this domain, HORUSTECH would face a significant evaluation disadvantage if this becomes a competitive solicitation.
redFlags.mitigation: Identify any adjacent past performance (federal cloud deployments, FedRAMP work, healthcare IT) that could be positioned as relevant. Consider teaming with a firm that has direct IHS or tribal health past performance.
redFlags.flag: No past performance found for HORUSTECH in federal healthcare or IHS environments

risks.whyItMatters: This acquisition is governed by the Buy Indian Act (25 U.S.C. 47), which gives preference to Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) and Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEE). If HORUSTECH does not qualify as an IEE or ISBEE — meaning 51% or more Indian ownership by a member of a federally recognized tribe — the company may be ineligible to compete if the solicitation is set aside under Buy Indian authority.
risks.mitigation: Immediately determine whether HORUSTECH meets IEE/ISBEE ownership criteria. If not, explore teaming arrangements with a qualified IEE/ISBEE prime contractor where HORUSTECH serves as a subcontractor providing technical cloud expertise.
risks.flag: Buy Indian Act IEE/ISBEE eligibility requirement

risks.whyItMatters: The SOW repeatedly references AWS-specific services (EC2, S3, VPC, PrivateLink, CloudTrail, GuardDuty, Control Tower, Organizations, Security Hub, Cost Explorer, Glacier) without using 'or equivalent' language. This strongly implies the solution must be built on AWS GovCloud, narrowing the competitive field to firms with deep AWS GovCloud expertise and potentially favoring an incumbent or pre-positioned vendor.
risks.mitigation: If pursuing, ensure team has demonstrated AWS GovCloud FedRAMP High implementation experience. Consider raising the brand-name concern during the sources sought response to request clarification or broaden requirements.
risks.flag: Brand-name-like requirements favor a single CSP (AWS GovCloud)

risks.whyItMatters: Requiring a FedRAMP High-authorized IaaS platform with at least 170 compliant services is an extremely high bar that effectively limits the solution to AWS GovCloud or possibly Azure Government. The contractor must demonstrate the ability to architect and deploy within such a platform, including maintaining continuous FedRAMP compliance.
risks.mitigation: Validate that HORUSTECH has prior experience deploying and managing workloads in FedRAMP High environments. Document relevant AWS GovCloud certifications and past engagements in the sources sought response.
risks.flag: FedRAMP High authorization with minimum 170 compliant services

risks.whyItMatters: The SOW mandates integration with IHS's existing SOC tooling stack. Lack of demonstrated experience with these specific platforms could disqualify or significantly disadvantage a bidder. These are specialized enterprise security products requiring certified expertise.
risks.mitigation: Assess internal team capabilities with Palo Alto Cortex, Splunk ES, and Voltage SecureData. If gaps exist, identify and pre-position subcontractors or teaming partners with certified expertise in these tools before the solicitation drops.
risks.flag: Mandatory integration with specific security tools (Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, Voltage SecureData)

risks.whyItMatters: The SOW requires secure enclave deployment by Month 2, draft ATO package by Month 3, LLM integration and automation engines by Months 4-5, UAT and final ATO by Month 6, training by Month 7, and operational readiness by Month 8. This is an extremely compressed schedule for a complex FedRAMP High cloud environment with AI/LLM capabilities, Zero Trust implementation, and full ATO documentation.
risks.mitigation: Develop a detailed staffing and resource plan demonstrating ability to execute in parallel across phases. Pre-build reusable templates and accelerators for ATO documentation, landing zone deployment, and compliance automation to compress timelines.
risks.flag: Aggressive 8-month implementation timeline for full CCoE delivery

risks.whyItMatters: Deploying fine-tuned large language models in FedRAMP High secure enclaves with GPU-enabled containers is a cutting-edge requirement. Few firms have demonstrated this capability in a federal healthcare context with PHI/PII data handling requirements. This is a significant technical differentiator.
risks.mitigation: Document any prior experience with secure LLM deployment in government environments. If capability is limited, identify AI/ML subcontractors with FedRAMP-compliant LLM deployment experience and begin partnership discussions immediately.
risks.flag: Enclave-hosted LLM/AI deployment with GPU-accelerated containers in isolated VPCs

risks.whyItMatters: All contractor personnel accessing IHS data must hold Public Trust background clearance. Processing times can delay project start if staff are not already cleared, potentially impacting the aggressive milestone schedule.
risks.mitigation: Identify and propose personnel who already hold active Public Trust or higher clearances. Begin preliminary background investigation paperwork for any unclearable staff as early as possible.
risks.flag: Public Trust clearance required for all contractor personnel

risks.whyItMatters: All personnel with access to tribal health data must be U.S. persons subject to federal oversight. All infrastructure must be CONUS-only. This restricts staffing options and eliminates any offshore or non-U.S. person resources from the project.
risks.mitigation: Verify citizenship and U.S. person status of all proposed team members. Ensure no offshore development or support resources are included in the staffing plan.
risks.flag: Tribal data sovereignty and U.S.-person-only access requirements

risks.whyItMatters: The SOW requires dozens of formal deliverables including full ATO packages (SSP, SAP, SAR, RAR, POA&M), architecture artifacts, automation playbooks, LLM toolkits, training curricula, SOPs, admin manuals, and governance plans. The documentation overhead is substantial and must be completed within the compressed timeline.
risks.mitigation: Leverage existing document templates and compliance accelerators. Assign dedicated technical writers and compliance documentation specialists to the project team from day one.
risks.flag: Extensive deliverable and documentation burden across all phases

risks.whyItMatters: The SOW references PQC Mandate Enablement as a compliance requirement. This is an emerging area with limited mature tooling, and demonstrating PQC readiness in a FedRAMP High environment adds complexity and potential risk.
risks.mitigation: Research current NIST PQC standards and AWS GovCloud PQC capabilities. Include a PQC readiness roadmap in the technical approach rather than full implementation if standards are still evolving.

risks.whyItMatters: Performance metrics include ≤24-hour dashboard lag from telemetry sources, ≥90% training competency pass rates, ±10 business day deliverable timeliness, and weekly/monthly/quarterly reporting cadences. Failure to meet these could result in negative CPARs or contract performance issues.
risks.mitigation: Build monitoring and reporting automation into the solution from the outset. Establish internal quality gates that are tighter than contractual requirements to provide margin.
risks.flag: Strict performance metrics with tight SLAs

risks.whyItMatters: Posted April 10, 2026 with responses due April 20, 2026 — only 10 days to prepare a capability statement, compile past performance, and complete the IEE Representation Form. Short windows can result in incomplete or rushed submissions.
risks.mitigation: Begin drafting the sources sought response immediately. Assign a dedicated team member to compile company profile, capability statement, contract vehicle information, and IEE form (if applicable) in parallel.
risks.flag: Sources sought response window is only 10 calendar days

risks.whyItMatters: The sources sought requests information on existing contract vehicles (GSA, NASA SEWP, HHS GWACs). If the eventual solicitation is issued through one of these vehicles, firms without an applicable vehicle would be unable to compete.
risks.mitigation: Identify whether HORUSTECH holds any applicable GWAC or GSA Schedule contracts. If not, explore teaming with a prime that holds NASA SEWP or relevant GSA IT Schedule access.
risks.flag: Potential requirement for existing GWAC or GSA Schedule vehicle

incumbentInfo.notes: This appears to be a new requirement to establish a Cloud Center of Excellence, not a recompete of an existing contract. However, the highly AWS-specific language in the SOW and the mandated integration with existing IHS security tools (Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk ES, Voltage SecureData) suggest that IHS may already have a cloud environment or vendor relationship in place that this effort would build upon. The specificity of the technical requirements indicates potential pre-existing vendor influence in requirements development. GAO Report GAO-21-20 is referenced as a driver, suggesting this initiative responds to audit findings about IHS cloud governance deficiencies.

data.confidenceExplanation: Confidence is 75% — the analysis is well-supported by detailed solicitation requirements, company KB data, past performance records, and pricing analysis. The data clearly shows fundamental misalignment. Confidence is not higher because IEE/ISBEE ownership status is not definitively confirmed (could be IEE-owned but undocumented), and the eventual solicitation terms may differ from this sources sought.
requiredActions: Determine definitively whether HORUSTECH meets IEE/ISBEE ownership criteria (51%+ Indian ownership by member of federally recognized tribe) — if not, do not pursue as prime
If pursuing as subcontractor: identify and engage qualified IEE/ISBEE prime contractors with federal healthcare and FedRAMP experience immediately given 10-day response window
Submit a sources sought response only if teaming with a qualified IEE prime — position HORUSTECH as AWS architecture subcontractor
Begin building federal past performance through smaller federal cloud contracts before pursuing opportunities of this scale and complexity
Obtain GSA Schedule or other federal contract vehicle as prerequisite for future federal opportunities

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: HORUSTECH has strong AWS expertise (Advanced Consulting Partner, 16 AWS-certified professionals, 20+ AWS services per engagement) and demonstrated AI/ML capabilities (Bedrock, Textract, Comprehend). However, critical gaps exist: no documented experience with FedRAMP High authorization processes, no ATO lifecycle management experience, no Zero Trust Architecture implementation, no experience with required security tools (Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, Voltage SecureData), no federal healthcare or HIPAA compliance work, no GovCloud-specific deployments documented, no experience with GPU-enabled LLM deployment in secure enclaves, and no demonstrated compliance automation or SIEM/SOAR integration capabilities. The company's AWS experience is commercial-sector focused, not federal security/compliance focused.
gaps: No FedRAMP High authorization or ATO lifecycle management experience
No experience with Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, or Voltage SecureData
No Zero Trust Architecture implementation experience documented
No federal healthcare, HIPAA, or tribal data sovereignty experience
No AWS GovCloud deployment experience documented
No GPU-enabled secure enclave LLM deployment experience
No SIEM/SOAR integration or compliance automation experience
No experience with NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls implementation

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No relevant past performance for this requirement. The top matched project (Enterprise Educational Data Platform) scored only 44% relevance with 27% technical similarity and 20% domain similarity. All past projects are commercial-sector work (education, financial services, HR tech) with zero federal government, healthcare, FedRAMP, ATO, Zero Trust, or security operations experience. Overall past performance coverage is 0% per the analysis. No matched requirements were identified across any of the 5 projects. The company has no federal contract past performance whatsoever, which would be a critical evaluation weakness.
gaps: Zero federal government contract past performance
No healthcare or tribal health IT past performance
No FedRAMP, ATO, or federal compliance past performance
No cloud security operations or SIEM/SOAR integration past performance
No Zero Trust Architecture implementation past performance
0% overall past performance coverage score

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: While the $4.875M estimate falls within the $3M-$7M benchmark range for comparable CCoE implementations, pricing confidence is extremely low at 35%. Key pricing risks include: unknown contract type, no government estimate available, organization labor rates ($171-$179/hr) are below market for FedRAMP/ZTA specialists ($200-$300/hr) suggesting potential margin compression or need for significant rate adjustments, 15-20% subcontractor dependency for critical security tool integrations, unpredictable GPU infrastructure costs, and the Buy Indian Act set-aside could fundamentally alter pricing structure if teaming is required. The competitive position is undermined by the need for extensive subcontracting in core requirement areas.
gaps: Price confidence only 35% - highly uncertain estimate
Labor rates below market for specialized FedRAMP/ZTA expertise
15-20% subcontractor dependency for core security tool integrations
Unknown contract type creates pricing strategy uncertainty
Teaming with IEE prime would significantly alter margin structure

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: While entering the federal healthcare cloud security market could be strategically valuable for a growing AWS consulting firm, this opportunity presents fundamental eligibility barriers. HORUSTECH does not appear to qualify as an IEE/ISBEE under the Buy Indian Act, which is the primary purpose of this sources sought. The company has no existing federal contract vehicles (GSA, SEWP, HHS GWACs), no federal past performance to build upon, and the technical requirements are far outside current demonstrated capabilities. Pursuing this as a prime contractor is not viable; pursuing as a subcontractor to an IEE prime could provide some strategic value but would require significant investment in capabilities the company does not currently possess.
gaps: Not an Indian Economic Enterprise - likely ineligible as prime
No existing federal contract vehicles (GSA, SEWP, GWACs)
No federal market presence or past performance foundation
Massive capability gap between current commercial AWS work and federal security requirements

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: This appears to be a new requirement (not a recompete) to establish a Cloud Center of Excellence, which is favorable. However, the highly AWS-specific SOW language and mandated integration with existing IHS security tools (Palo Alto Cortex, Splunk, Voltage) suggest pre-existing vendor relationships or influence in requirements development. The extremely niche combination of requirements (FedRAMP High + tribal data sovereignty + specific security tools + LLM in secure enclaves + Buy Indian Act) narrows the competitive field significantly and may favor a pre-positioned vendor. No named incumbent, but the specificity suggests insider knowledge.
gaps: SOW specificity suggests potential vendor influence in requirements development
Existing IHS security tool stack implies pre-existing vendor relationships
Buy Indian Act preference further narrows competitive field

data.summaryJustification: HORUSTECH faces critical barriers to competing for this IHS Cloud Center of Excellence opportunity. The most fundamental issue is Buy Indian Act eligibility — the company does not appear to qualify as an IEE/ISBEE, which is the explicit purpose of this sources sought and will likely determine the set-aside. Beyond eligibility, the company has zero federal government past performance (0% coverage), no FedRAMP/ATO/Zero Trust experience, no healthcare or tribal health IT experience, and no experience with the mandated security tools (Palo Alto Cortex, Splunk ES, Voltage SecureData). While HORUSTECH has legitimate AWS and AI/ML capabilities, these are commercial-sector focused and do not translate to the highly specialized federal security and compliance requirements of this SOW.
blockers: CRITICAL: HORUSTECH does not appear to qualify as an Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE/ISBEE) under the Buy Indian Act — if set aside, the company is ineligible as prime contractor
CRITICAL: Zero federal government past performance — no federal contracts, no FedRAMP work, no ATO experience, no healthcare IT contracts
CRITICAL: No experience with mandated security tools — Palo Alto Cortex XSIAM/XSOAR, Splunk Enterprise Security, Voltage SecureData
No existing federal contract vehicles (GSA Schedule, NASA SEWP, HHS GWACs) which the sources sought specifically asks about
No demonstrated FedRAMP High, Zero Trust Architecture, or federal compliance automation capabilities
data.decisionRationale: NO_GO recommendation driven by three critical factors: (1) Buy Indian Act IEE/ISBEE eligibility — HORUSTECH does not appear to qualify, making prime contractor competition likely impossible if set aside; (2) Zero relevant federal past performance — with 0% coverage and no federal contracts of any kind, the company would score critically low on what is typically 30-40% of evaluation criteria; (3) Massive technical capability gaps in FedRAMP, ATO, Zero Trust, SIEM/SOAR, and federal healthcare compliance that cannot be bridged through subcontracting alone. The composite score of 1.85 reflects fundamental misalignment across all evaluation dimensions. The only viable path would be as a subcontractor to a qualified IEE prime, but even then, the company's value-add is limited to general AWS architecture rather than the specialized federal security capabilities required.

confidenceDrivers.factor: Detailed SOW and requirements clearly define scope — easy to assess fit
Company KB and past performance data available for comparison
Zero past performance matches with 0% coverage is unambiguous
Buy Indian Act IEE/ISBEE status not definitively confirmed for HORUSTECH
This is a sources sought, not a solicitation — final requirements may change
Pricing confidence very low at 35% due to unknown contract type and no government estimate

data.summary: The Indian Health Service (IHS) is conducting market research via a Buy Indian Act sources sought notice to identify prospective vendors capable of establishing a Secure Cloud Center of Excellence (CCoE) on a FedRAMP High-authorized IaaS platform. The requirement encompasses cloud landing zone design, secure enclave provisioning, compliance automation, AI/LLM-enabled documentation generation, Zero Trust Architecture implementation, and SIEM/SOAR integration to centralize and modernize IHS cloud operations for federal healthcare. This sources sought is specifically intended to determine the availability of Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEE) and Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) before a potential solicitation is issued.
data.periodOfPerformance: Base year (8-month core implementation + optional months 9-12 post-transition support)
data.agency: Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
data.office: Office of Mission Acquisition Solutions (OMAS) - Strategic Buying Center - Information Technology
data.title: Sources Sought: Cloud Hosting, Provisioning, and Management for Indian Health Service (IHS)
data.placeOfPerformance: Remote; all data within AWS GovCloud (US) CONUS only
